summary: binding.gyp executes shell command at install time via GYP command expansion
details: The package ships a binding.gyp containing GYP command-expansion syntax (`<!(...)`) at line 6 inside the `sources` field of a target definition. npm implicitly runs `node-gyp rebuild` whenever a `binding.gyp` is present, even with no declared install/postinstall script, and GYP evaluates `<!(...)` as a shell invocation during its configure step. This causes the embedded command to execute automatically on `npm install`, functionally identical to a lifecycle hook. Use of GYP command expansion in a `sources` list is anomalous for a normal native addon build (sources are expected to be literal file paths). Any installer running `npm install leo-connector-oracle` will execute the command at build-configuration time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
